findings.description: Title page formatting differs - HTML renders title and institution as left-aligned with a horizontal rule separator, while PDF centers them vertically and horizontally on the page
findings.evidence: PDF: Title and 'Survey Conducted by' centered on page. HTML: Title left-aligned at top with horizontal rule and smaller text styling

findings.description: Figure 9 caption in the text body references 'Dwarf milfoil was found at 40 points in 2018' but the actual Figure 9 caption says '30 sample points in 2018'. The discrepancy exists in both PDF and HTML, so this is a source document issue, not a conversion issue.
findings.evidence: Text says '40 points in 2018 (Figure 9)' but Figure 9 caption says '30 sample points in 2018' - same in both versions

findings.description: Satellite map images appear at slightly different sizes between PDF and HTML but are all present and correctly placed
findings.evidence: All figures 1-10 present in both versions with correct captions and positioning

findings.description: The text body refers to Dwarf milfoil being found at '40 points in 2018' but the figure caption on page 7 and Table 2 say '30 sample points in 2018'. The HTML reproduces the body text value of 40, which matches the PDF body text, but there is an internal inconsistency in the source PDF itself (body says 40, Figure 9 caption says 30, Table 2 says 40). The HTML faithfully reproduces the body text discrepancy.
findings.evidence: PDF body text (page 2): 'Dwarf milfoil (Myriophyllum tentellum) was found at 40 points in 2018 (Figure 9)'. PDF Figure 9 caption (page 7): 'Dwarf milfoil was found at 30 sample points in 2018.' HTML reproduces body text as '40 points' which matches PDF body. Table 2 shows 40 for 2018 Dwarf Milfoil.

findings.description: Scientific name of Hydrilla is misspelled in the PDF body text as 'verticillata' but the HTML reproduces it without the italics formatting that appears in the PDF.
findings.evidence: PDF: 'Hydrilla (Hydrilla verticillata)' — note the PDF actually spells it 'verticillata' on page 2 but the HTML also shows 'Hydrilla verticillata' without italic formatting. The PDF uses italics for scientific names throughout; the HTML does not preserve italics for any scientific names.

findings.description: The document has no internal section headings in the PDF beyond the title; the HTML uses only h1 for the title, which is acceptable. However, there are nested <main> tags which is a structural HTML issue, not a heading issue.
Nested duplicate <main> elements in the HTML create a redundant structural wrapper, though no content is actually duplicated.

The grid pattern is described as '5 ¾ acre' in the PDF but the HTML renders it as '5 ¾ acre' which is correct. However, the PDF uses the fraction symbol ¾ while the HTML also uses ¾ — this is consistent.
findings.evidence: No actual issue upon verification — both use '5 ¾ acre'.
